# Title: File CPSC certificates electronically starting July 8, 2026

Title: File CPSC certificates electronically starting July 8, 2026

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GWCJUJ9WUZNRYVGN

Markdown Content:
This article applies to selling in: **United States**

The new requirement affects regulated imported products shipped to fulfillment centers in the US.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) has announced new requirements for submitting certificates of compliance at the border which go into effect on July 8, 2026. All shipments already in transit or at the border will be required to meet these new requirements starting July 8, 2026. Review the information below to prepare your shipments and avoid delays.

Starting July 8, 2026, you must electronically file Certificate of Compliance data for regulated imported consumer products at the time of entry. This changes _how_ you submit certificate data — not _which_ products require certification.

**Key deadlines:**

**Key deadlines:**

*   **July 8, 2026** — Regulated consumer products not imported through Foreign Trade Zones (FTZ)
*   **January 8, 2027** — Regulated consumer products imported through Foreign Trade Zones

**Does this apply to your products?**

This requirement applies to finished consumer products subject to CPSC rules that you are importing for consumption or warehousing. To find out if your products require certification, visit [eFiling – CPSC’s Modern Approach for Filing Certificate Data](https://cpsc.gov/eFiling) and use the [Regulatory Robot](https://www.cpsc.gov/Business--Manufacturing/Regulatory-Robot/Safer-Products-Start-Here) tool.

**What you need to file**

When importing regulated products, you must electronically file the following seven data elements at entry:

1.   Product ID
2.   Citation codes
3.   Date of manufacture
4.   Place of manufacture
5.   Product test date
6.   Testing laboratory
7.   Point of contact

**How to submit**

**How to submit**

You have two options. Contact your logistics provider or customs broker to determine which method is right for you:

*   **Option A** — Your customs broker transmits the data directly to the U.S. Customs and Border Protection.
*   **Option B** — You enter the data in the CPSC's Product Registry prior to entry. Then, provide the certificate identifiers to your customs broker.

**What happens if you don't comply**

Non-compliant shipments will face border delays and holds, slowing down the time it takes for your products to reach customers. Compliant importers benefit from fewer examinations and faster customs clearance.

**Steps to take now**

**Steps to take now**

1.   Visit [eFiling – CPSC’s Modern Approach for Filing Certificate Data](https://cpsc.gov/eFiling) and use the [Regulatory Robot](https://www.cpsc.gov/Business--Manufacturing/Regulatory-Robot/Safer-Products-Start-Here) tool to confirm whether your products require certification.
2. Review the filing requirements before the deadline — July 8, 2026.

3. Collect the necessary data.

4. Set up electronic filing with the U.S. Customs Border Protection (CBP) through your customs broker or file certificate data directly with the CPSC Product Registry.

5. Work with your customs broker to test your eFiling process ahead of the deadline.

For case-specific questions, consult your customs broker or legal counsel. You can also explore the [Amazon Customs & Trade](https://spcentral.amazon.com/nlp/customs-and-trade) or the [Amazon Service Provider Network](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gspn/searchpage/International%20Shipping) for additional support.

## Resources and support

*   Full details and resources: [https://www.cpsc.gov/eFiling](https://www.cpsc.gov/eFiling)
*   Questions or training materials: [eFilingSupport@cpsc.gov](http://eFilingSupport@cpsc.gov/)
